text.en_US: Those lakes don't look natural at all.
Crossworlds can be so unrealistic.
This place is from a mod though.
No wonder it doesn't follow the normal design standards!
A doughnut-shaped pond, perfectly in line with other odd ponds.
I wonder what it all looks like from above?
Me too! I would install the freecam mod again but it's glitchy and breaks too many things.
It's a shame there is no working minimap of this place.
I think I'm finally going to do it, I'll bring back the fishing system as a mod.
It was never implemented beyond early versions of Autumn's Rise though.
You'd need to come up with a lot of new fish for the rest of the biomes.
True, and they would all ideally have different catching mechanics.
How do you plan to develop something like that?
I'll probably start by taking each of these small ponds here and populating them with different modded fish.
This really is the perfect location to work on this project!
Can you please come down already? I want to explore this place!
Those roads don't go anywhere. It just loops back around.
Wait, really? Is it not finished or something?
Probably. I think this area was just a random map file some modder found.
Well that's a shame! Hopefully more modders can learn to make maps like this.
So this whole place was made by a single modder?
It's like a whole valley with water features and everything! How does one person do it all?
There's a fan-made map editor we use in the modding community. It's quite powerful.
Wow, really? Why aren't there more maps like this from dozens of modders then?
I have no idea... So much effort was put into the editor tool yet nobody really uses it.
It's probably a lack of documentation paired with a lack of impressive projects made with it to inspire people.
I heard someone is working on the documentation though, let's hope that leads more potential modders down the path of map creation.
How's your mod project coming along? It's been a year since you released the original trailer!
Ugh... To be honest that trailer was faking a lot of things.
There were no functioning navigation maps, for one. And the enemies crash the game if you start combat.
Were you ever able to figure that stuff out? Surely there is an easy fix!
Well... I kind of gave up after a while. I don't think I have it in me to finish the original project.
Aww, that's such a shame! Why does this keep happening to all the big Crossworlds mod projects?
Honestly I don't know. I just lost the motivation at some point.
One day, I'm going to learn how to create my own maps for Crossworlds mods.
What's stopping you from getting started right now?
I need to come up with ideas for my project first.
I'm thinking about expanding on the story of some of the minor Crossworlds characters.
With full backstories complete with cutscenes and playable flashback sequences!
That sounds ambitious. Why not start with something small, like following the map editor tutorial?
Or just make a basic map like the one we're setting in right now? It's good practice.
A lot of modders have plenty of ideas but quit during the process of learning the basics from what I've seen.
I only want to work on my project though. I just don't really know where to get started...

message.en_US: These shops were obviously just copied from Bergen Trail somewhere.
Those lifeless NPCs just stand there, without any routine or further programming.
It's honestly quite unsettling, even more so than regular Crossworlds NPCs.
I know these shops are just filler decoration and don't serve any real purpose...
But I actually just bought 20 \c[3]Frosted Carrots\c[0] at this shop for that one quest I never completed.
Do you think it will cause any issues or glitches to use these items back in vanilla Crossworlds?
I'm actually really worried now!\. Is my inventory going to get corrupted?\. Are these items going to get flagged somehow?\. What do I do?!
[appears to be AFK]
It's quite neat, you know? To have a peaceful little area with no enemies or game mechanics like this.
Only visited by people that found the hidden mod server containing this map.
It's a shame Crossworlds doesn't have anything like this... Nowhere to just enjoy beautiful weather with the avatar immersion settings maxed out.
See that landmark?
The teleporters don't actually function, nor are we in a location that actually shows up on the world map.
How do those players keep walking out? None of them will even acknowledge me!
Do you think Instatainment simulates fake players to create the illusion of a high population?
Have they ALWAYS been doing this?!
You've wandered into this place too, huh? Looking through some unused mod files?
Either way, welcome to our little getaway. It's a peaceful place.
You know, Bergen Trail as a setting actually makes for a nice flat, lake-filled landscape.
No snow or mountains, and it's easier on the eyes than Autumn's Rise.
It's a shame we get to see so little of it in Crossworlds.
